key_insights.insight: Compromising your core values and authentic self for a relationship leads to resentment and exhaustion.
key_insights.quote: If you're having to change who you are to make someone happy, I just don't feel like that's real love.

key_insights.insight: True alignment in a relationship means both partners accept each other's values and visions.
key_insights.quote: But if it's not in alignment with her values, her vision, her lifestyle, my values, my vision, my lifestyle, and we don't fully accept who we are, then we shouldn't be together.

key_insights.insight: The willingness to walk away from a relationship can bring peace because you are not attached.
key_insights.quote: The only way this works is I'm willing to walk away at any moment...And it brings me peace because I'm not attached. I'm committed. I'm holding it loosely.

key_insights.insight: It is important to set personal priorities (like health and mission) as a foundation for a fulfilling relationship.
key_insights.quote: My number one priority needs to be my health. Because without my health, I can't fully show up for my number two priority, which is my mission or my calling from God.

key_insights.insight: Abandoning yourself in relationships stems from a fear of losing love, which is not real love.
key_insights.quote: I was afraid and I lacked the really the self-confidence to step away because I was afraid of losing love. But it's not real love if it's inauthentic.

key_insights.insight: Focus on adjusting your flaws rather than changing your core personality for someone else.
key_insights.quote: If you've got flaws, adjust those, improve those...But if it's something that's at my core is my personality, I'm not changing for anyone.

actionable_advice.advice: Define your core values and priorities before entering a committed relationship.
actionable_advice.how_to: Spend time reflecting on what is truly important to you (e.g., health, personal mission, family) and rank them in order of importance.  Communicate these to a potential partner early on.

actionable_advice.advice: Cultivate a willingness to walk away from relationships that compromise your core values.
actionable_advice.how_to: Recognize that staying true to yourself is more important than maintaining a relationship. Build self-confidence and self-worth independent of external validation.

actionable_advice.advice: Prioritize your health and personal mission.
actionable_advice.how_to: Dedicate time each day to activities that improve your physical and mental well-being, as well as activities that align with your purpose or calling.

memorable_stories: The guest describes telling his current partner that she will never be his number one priority and her positive reaction to that transparency.
The guest shares his history of abandoning himself and wearing 'masks' in previous relationships to maintain peace, highlighting the negative outcomes.
contrarian_claims: The guest asserts that partners should not be each other's number one priority; personal health and mission should come first.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 69 - People Pleasing Is Hurting You: Lewis Howes
